title: Missing model field, missing tools field, and generic description
why_it_matters: No model field (inherits default). No tools field (inherits all tools — overpermissioned). Description is generic without trigger context.
suggested_fix: Add model: sonnet and tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash] to frontmatter. Update description with trigger context.
notes: Merged 1A missing-model + 1B missing-tools into single finding. Both confirmed by reading actual frontmatter.

title: Non-standard color field used
why_it_matters: color field present on dependency-manager and documentation-expert but absent from all other non-GSD agents. Inconsistent — either adopt project-wide or remove from these two.
suggested_fix: Remove color: yellow from non-GSD agents, or add color to all agents for consistency.
notes: Downgraded confidence from 90 to 70. Color IS used across all GSD agents — it is a project convention, not an error. Only inconsistent for these 2 non-GSD agents.

title: Model mismatch: 990-line agent with sonnet

title: Model borderline: sonnet for complex framework agent

suggested_fix: Consider upgrading to model: opus.

title: Missing AskUserQuestion for checkpoint confirmations
why_it_matters: Agent discusses checkpoint confirmations but tools list is [Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Edit, Write] with no AskUserQuestion. deployment-engineer.md DOES list AskUserQuestion, confirming it is a valid tool.
suggested_fix: Add AskUserQuestion to tools if checkpoints need user input.
notes: Reduced confidence from 75 to 70 — checkpoints may be handled by parent orchestrator, not agent itself.

title: Agent context budget large (42KB, not 51KB)
why_it_matters: 42KB prompt — largest agent in the ecosystem. Risks quality degradation on weaker models.
suggested_fix: Consider splitting into sub-agents or chunked processing. At minimum, add model: opus.
notes: CORRECTED: 43207 bytes = ~42KB, not 51KB as originally claimed. Finding still valid — it is the largest agent.

title: Debugging Domain Cluster (3 agents, 25-30% overlap)
why_it_matters: Three agents targeting overlapping debugging/RCA problem space. All have model: sonnet and similar tool sets. Boundaries are scope-based: debugger=code-level, error-detective=log patterns, devops-troubleshooter=production incidents. But CLAUDE.md only triggers 'systematic-debugging' skill, not these agents.
suggested_fix: Create decision matrix in CLAUDE.md: debugger=code-level, error-detective=log patterns, devops-troubleshooter=production incidents.
notes: Confirmed by reading all 3 agent frontmatters. All have model: sonnet, tools include Read/Write/Edit/Bash/Grep.

title: Security Domain Cluster (3 agents, 20-25% overlap)
why_it_matters: All three perform vulnerability assessment. security-auditor has model: opus, security-engineer has model: opus, penetration-tester has model: opus. CLAUDE.md only triggers security-auditor. The other two are orphaned.
suggested_fix: Clarify: auditor=code review, engineer=infrastructure, pen-tester=attack simulation. Add trigger conditions to CLAUDE.md.
notes: Confirmed all 3 have model: opus. Only security-auditor is in CLAUDE.md triggers.

title: Documentation Domain Cluster (3 agents, 20-25% overlap)
why_it_matters: All write documentation. technical-writer (sonnet), documentation-expert (no model), markdown-syntax-formatter (sonnet). CLAUDE.md only triggers documentation-expert.
suggested_fix: technical-writer=user guides, documentation-expert=system/API docs, markdown-syntax-formatter=format fixes only.
notes: Confirmed. documentation-expert is in CLAUDE.md triggers; the other two are orphaned.

title: Frontend Domain Cluster (3 agents, 15-20% overlap)

suggested_fix: Document: frontend-developer=implementation, react-performance-optimization=perf issues, ui-ux-designer=design decisions.

title: GSD ecosystem (12 agents) isolated from CLAUDE.md standard triggers
why_it_matters: Complete parallel ecosystem with zero CLAUDE.md integration. GSD agents ARE integrated via /gsd:* commands (verified: plan-phase, execute-phase, debug, map-codebase, research-phase all spawn GSD agents). But CLAUDE.md Section 7 has no entry pointing users to GSD commands.
suggested_fix: Document GSD as alternative orchestration mode in CLAUDE.md Section 7 with clear trigger conditions (e.g., 'Multi-phase project' -> /gsd:new-project).
notes: Extended: GSD agents are NOT orphaned from their OWN ecosystem — they are wired via /gsd commands. The issue is CLAUDE.md doesn't mention /gsd commands as triggers.

title: Multi-role overlap with security-auditor, test-engineer, performance-engineer
why_it_matters: code-reviewer (model: sonnet) performs security, testing, and perf checks — overlapping with 3 specialist agents (all opus-level). Acts as generalist gate.
suggested_fix: Clarify code-reviewer as primary gate; specialists for deep dives.
notes: Confirmed. code-reviewer is the only code-quality agent in CLAUDE.md triggers.

title: Invocations file tracks skills only, not agents
why_it_matters: All 27 records are type: 'skill' (pr-review, pr-retro, skill-audit, hook-ecosystem-audit, deep-plan). No agent invocations tracked at all. Cannot verify actual agent usage patterns.
suggested_fix: Add agent invocation tracking to the invocation logging system, or verify agents are tracked elsewhere.
notes: CORRECTED: Original finding said 'null agent_name — data corruption'. Actual issue is different: no agent_name field exists because records are skill invocations, not agent invocations. Not data corruption — missing feature.

title: CLAUDE.md references built-in agents without .md definitions
why_it_matters: CLAUDE.md triggers reference Explore and Plan agents but these are Claude Code built-ins, not custom .claude/agents/ definitions. No confusion in practice but worth documenting.
suggested_fix: Add note to CLAUDE.md that Explore/Plan are built-in agents, not in .claude/agents/.
notes: Confirmed.

title: Duplicate GSD agents in global/ directory with divergent definitions
why_it_matters: 11 GSD agents are duplicated in .claude/agents/global/ with divergent frontmatter (global versions lack skills: field, have different description formatting). Creates ambiguity about which version is canonical. Global versions may override or conflict with project-level versions.
suggested_fix: Determine canonical location. If global/ is for cross-project use, sync frontmatter. If redundant, remove global/ copies.
notes: NEW finding missed by all 3 scan agents. Discovered during verification when checking color field across all files.
